tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I'm curious about what kind of information you have stored about me right now. Can you show me what memories or preferences you've got saved?

Hi there! I've checked my memory storage, and it looks like there are currently no memories or preferences saved about you yet. 

This is a fresh start! If you'd like, you can:

- **Ask me to remember something** — Just tell me something you'd like me to store (like your preferences, interests, or anything else you'd like me to keep in mind for future conversations).
- **Share preferences as we chat** — I'll ask if you'd like me to remember anything important.

Is there anything you'd like me to store for future reference?

Yeah, actually could you remember that I prefer getting straight to the facts? I don't really need the extra fluff or lengthy explanations—just give me the key points and I'll ask follow-ups if I need more detail.
Got it — I'll keep our conversations short and to the point. Thanks for the heads up!

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User prefers concise, direct communication - give them the key facts upfront without lengthy explanations. They will ask follow-up questions if they need more detail."}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "memory_id": "mem_8f3d2a1c", "stored_text": "User prefers concise, direct communication - give them the key facts upfront without lengthy explanations. They will ask follow-up questions if they need more detail."}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Alright, so now I want to test if that memory you just saved actually shows up when you search for it. Can you pull up that preference I just gave you about wanting concise info?

They will ask follow-up questions if they need more detail.", "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "query": "prefers concise direct communication key facts", "results_count": 1}

messages.content: Works. Found your preference — it's stored and retrievable.
Can you delete that memory you just stored? I want to test if removal works too.

Cool, that worked. So the system is actually functional — add, search, and delete all work. I'm satisfied.

[TASK_END]
Glad the tests passed. Let me know if you need anything else.

Cool, that worked. So the system is actually functional — add, search, and delete all work. I'm satisfied.

[TASK_END]
Cool. Thanks for testing it out.

structured_turns.goal_text: Check what memories are currently stored about the user
structured_turns.user_message: Hey, I'm curious about what kind of information you have stored about me right now. Can you show me what memories or preferences you've got saved?
structured_turns.assistant_message:
Hi there! I've checked my memory storage, and it looks like there are currently no memories or preferences saved about you yet. 

This is a fresh start! If you'd like, you can:

- **Ask me to remember something** — Just tell me something you'd like me to store (like your preferences, interests, or anything else you'd like me to keep in mind for future conversations).
- **Share preferences as we chat** — I'll ask if you'd like me to remember anything important.

Is there anything you'd like me to store for future reference?

structured_turns.goal_text: Store a preference about how they want information presented
structured_turns.user_message: Yeah, actually could you remember that I prefer getting straight to the facts? I don't really need the extra fluff or lengthy explanations—just give me the key points and I'll ask follow-ups if I need more detail.
